item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad to see the continuing adventures of D-11424, although I fear he has found his calling in the meat maze.

So much fridge logic to unpack here, deeply unsettling and weirdly comforting in equal measure. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While, as is custom, I like the exploration log and D-11424, I feel like the D-class's presence is explained far too little.

He is presumed dead until the next exploration log very casually mentions him walking around. Where's the article's commentary on what happened to D-11424 and what the Foundation's going to do about him (surely he has a bunch of useful info waiting to be retrieved)? How is he treating a large bloody worm as a scared pet?

The ending was a good twist though. Still solid overall +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
D-11424, unintentional protector of friendly giant bloodworms and scourge of nasty cannibal graboid type worms. And potentially the unwitting cause of countless otherwise-unexplained pregnancies.

Presumably, he knows exactly when to, and when not to walk with rhythm.

Is there supposed to be a bit of an Earthbound/Giygas vibe going on here or is it just a thematic resemblance?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it possible for an SCP to combine creepy body horror with heart-warming fuzziness at the same time? Because this is what I get from reading this SCP here.
does 'the worm'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
if a user -wants- to use their phone in portrait mode, they shouldn't be punished with an article that looks like ass.

Ha! No, they should not. Perhaps I'll find an alternative set-up that will actually work across platforms and modes. Until such time, I'll begrudgingly revert.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't use my phone in landscape mode. It makes the keyboard hard to use. Or what I should say is, if a user -wants- to use their phone in portrait mode, they shouldn't be punished with an article that looks like ass.

The wiki defaults to Verdana, and if you're insistent on deviating from the standard format, I'd stick with that. Here's what that header would look like in Verdana, and here's what it'd look like in bolded Verdana.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh, it looks fine on mobile to me…so long as I'm in landscape mode. If there's any way to fix that, I'm all ears, but everything I try doesn't seem to affect that part of the layout.

…and I really like how Impact looks. I probably just have horrible taste though. Any suggestions for an agreeable font?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, if you're going to use a kaktus-esque fancy-font header, please don't use Impact (or something very much like it) of all things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not to mention they just plain don't look very good on mobile.

This is why I generally oppose fancy formatting — it's not reliable across platforms, screen sizes, browsers, etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don’t understand the changes here. They’re honestly distracting from an otherwise great article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm surprised I didn't port my original comment to this re-upload. Weird…

The gist of it, iirc, was "This is awesome, one of my new favorites. But I wonder if I could get a word-of-god explanation from the author."

I came back to say thanks for introducing me to Streetlight Manifesto, Shaggy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So they're giant skull sperms?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So from what I can construe the -1's can be transformed into -2's, and the -2's must be guided to some orb where they transform into humans? And the d class is guiding the -2's to safety. This is a hella good concept. I don't have any nit picks or criticisms.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I could totally see Foundation monitors being designed to output massive amounts of light. We have a lot of stuff that would come in handy against.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope. They're already inside it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kind of gross, in my taste. At least I'm still human.

Anyways, all joking aside, this was well-written. It's not more than a few times where you get to see articles like this with a creative setting attached to it. I especially liked the logs, and how it describes (from view of perspective) the inner core areas. Still' trying to figure out the end log, at the last part. None the less, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Roger. I'll give this a reread later. I appreciate the change, but that wasn't my only drawback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So glad you were able to get the credit for this that you deserved. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved it! Just one small grammatical error I spotted:

"Instances will wander SCP-2385 with no apparent direction"

should be

"Instances will wander into SCP-2385 with no apparent direction"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Most of the water you put into your body comes from the food you eat. A piece of raw meat is filled with blood and other fluids. A whole cave made out of human flesh would probably have a lot of dripping.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One nipick….

Why didn't the D-class dehydrate? I mean, what is he drinking?

This breaks my suspension of disbelief. But still, I upvoted. Hopefully it gets fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Petrograd, he changed it. Give it a look again pal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Simply change the line to state that the brightness was not in fact so severe, so they're unsure as to how the personnel were blinded. Not sure how to word this, at the moment.

Maybe something like, "At the point, luminance levels increased until the remote camera failed. Researchers observing the video feed received irreversible retinal damage. It is believed that this was a cognitohazardous effect." (You can adapt the original language, though, I'm just on my phone.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like that. feex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it! I feel like the information in the last addenda could have been spread out a little more, no pun intended, such a short paragraph quickly wrapping everything up after longish exploration logs felt a little odd to me. A minor quibble, though. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I could perhaps add a more on the D (criminal background or somesuch) if you think that would bring that out more.

I am going to use a food metaphor here. The salad dressing brings life and flavor to a salad; however, if you put too much you drown it. I think this article is well rounded and each part plays an important role in the whole picture, and I feel satiated with what you provided to me.

As for the TV stuff, I'd say just to take the blinding away. Another good way to fix this without taking the "should not be perceived", then how about you make the researchers (anyone who sees it) unable to remember1what they saw, yet, they get an overwhelming feeling when attempting to recall it. Hell, it might even be an indescribable feeling; just, overwhelming would be fine.

Footnotes
1. antimemetics

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To address some concerns:

Probably going to be spoilery, but whatever.

Petrograd

If you were going for the "mine eyes have seen the glory, no man looks on the face of God" type crap, fine, but that's not what it reads as now, and you should reword it.

+ show block
– hide block

Definitely what I was trying to convey. I see a couple possible fixes for this:

  • Go with the original plan, which was to [DATA EXPUNGED]. I feel like that's a cop out, so unless there's some kind of consensus here that it'd be an improvement, I'm reluctant to use it.
  • Simply change the line to state that the brightness was not in fact so severe, so they're unsure as to how the personnel were blinded. Not sure how to word this, at the moment.

Even if that is what you're going for, there's nothing in the rest of the SCP that thematically matches with that ability, so it reads just like random unrelated junk to make this more spooooooky.

+ show block
– hide block

Spoopy is what I was really hoping to avoid. I simply wanted to leave a blank space to add some mystery. It made sense to me that this would be something that can/should not be perceived. The whole thing is centered around rebirth, so I hope that part doesn't seem off. Between the behavior of the -1s vs -2s:

(-1s: Violent and gluttonous. Seek to gratify their own needs at the expense of others.)
(-2s Show compassion, have sense of community are willing to sacrifice to benefit another. Ready to 'pass on')

The human tissue bit, the d-class's dialogue, the birth ending, the physical appearance of the -1s/-2s, the conversion of a -1 into a -2… it all should add up to make it clear that what was 'witnessed' was a kind of (as you quite aptly put it) 'majesty cognitohazard' as the instance passes over.

I'm almost certain that part fits with the rest of the skip, but is there anything I could do to make it clearer? I wanted to avoid the blatant "The -1s/-2s DNA matches deceased individuals." or somesuch. All ears.

I would rather have been left in complete mystery then given what was said in the last addendum.

This has mainly been about the 'flash of light' bit. I'm not sure if you're still referring to that here, or if by 'last addendum' you mean the kicker at the end? If it's the latter, I think I'll have to leave it as-is. Most people enjoyed that bit.

FaustoV

+ show block
– hide block

If you take in count the D's most obvious backstory, you can portrait it as some kind of twisted and yet warm form of redemption.

Very much this. There was originally going to be a companion Tale exploring that. This interpretation further fits the theme I was going for. I could perhaps add a more on the D (criminal background or somesuch) if you think that would bring that out more.

BananaRepublic

I still like it, but the lack of that picture bugs me.

It's displaying for me. I have no idea how to fix this. If anyone knows what could possibly be the issue, I'd be grateful for some technical support.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the way this is handled. almost each part of the article (except the television part1) leads us to a final realization. This is not extremely criptyc, nor easy enough to make me feel like you're treating me like child. The imagery (and those images) are excellent, and probably my favorite part is the D-class adapting to this enviroment to fullfill a purpose. If you take in count the D's most obvious backstory, you can portrait it as some kind of twisted and yet warm form of redemption.

TL;DR: this is lovely.

Footnotes
1. I share Petrograd's concerns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved the original version, and I like this version even more. You did an excellent job of expanding the concept and adding depth to the narrative. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I still like it, but the lack of that picture bugs me. Not the article's fault, just, annoys me.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My main critique:

The luminosity increased sharply, and caused irreversible retinal damage to researchers viewing the live feed.

This, as written, is patently ridiculous. Computer/video monitors aren't magic, and unless the Foundation is intentionally building monitors with high-powered search lights in them for the express purposes of blinding researchers, the worst that can happen is that the screen goes white and hits its maximum brightness. If you were going for the "mine eyes have seen the glory, no man looks on the face of God" type crap, fine, but that's not what it reads as now, and you should reword it.

Even if that is what you're going for, there's nothing in the rest of the SCP that thematically matches with that ability, so it reads just like random unrelated junk to make this more spooooooky. (Side note: If blinding people with the majesty cognitohazard-style is thematically out in left field, specifically being able to overclock mundane computer monitors isn't even in the tri-county area, let alone the ballpark.)

Overall… No vote for now, but I'm actively thinking on it. (Edit: On the downside, this is built out some of the oldest tropes on the wiki. It's a weird tunnel system that can't be reached from other angles and is made of human tissue and full of weird worm things. If we're looking for SCPs which meet at least 2 out of those 4, there are dozens.) I still enjoyed the building up of the story in the tunnels, but I feel like I would rather have been left in complete mystery then given what was said in the last addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shame that whole situation had to happen. Great scip though.

+1

item.title: Deja vu, anyone?
item.content:encoded:
DISCLAIMER:

I thought this was already posted
Well it was. Here's the thing.

This article is a rewrite of one from 'xenomorph666'. The original article is below, it was deleted. The user offered it to me for a rewrite, because I had an idea on how to work it. They seemed to be cool, and seemed like they really, really, really wanted to contribute to the wiki, thus I offered them co-authorship.

After a couple brainstorming sessions where I explained our goals, we got started. It quickly became apparent that they did not understand the thrust of the article, and each part they were assigned had to be culled. This was partially due to their inability, partially due to their failure to grasp the concept whatsoever.

The final product contains 100% my writing, 100% my vision. Their contribution was inspiring me with the title 'meat maze'. and that is literally it. Because I am far, far too nice, I let them post it anyways, since their article inspired it in the first place. I had zero issues sitting back as co-author, as I hoped they'd at least learn from the process and successfully contribute a solo work in the future.

That was of course until the user decided they needed to troll the wiki, including vandalising several sandboxes to include porn (mine included) and sending users porn and such through PMs.

I'm not comfortable covering for them anymore. They had no part of this. With permission from the Rewrite Team, I reposted.

Original Author Post
Well someday soon my friend, this life will come to an end. But we --can't-- just get in line again.

The pre-rewrite version of this, for comparison:

+ show block
– hide block

Item #: SCP-XXXX

Object Class: Euclid

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-XXXX is located and contained in Area-40. The entrance to SCP-XXXX is constructed of reinforced steel with an electro-release lock mechanism. Five armed guards must be stationed near SCP-XXXX in case of the escape of SCP-XXXX-1.

Since ██-██-████ , no human subjects are allowed to enter SCP-XXXX under any circumstances.

Description: SCP-XXXX is an long sets of hallways constructed entirely out of organic matters. The length and size of the structure is unknown, but it is estimated to be ██ km long. SCP-XXXX appears to have an abnormally high regenerative ability as it is able to regenerate large chunks of tissues in seconds.

Creatures designated as SCP-XXXX-1 will form on the wall of SCP-XXXX from time to time. SCP-XXXX-1 specimens resembles a large, red, skinless, humanoid fetus with an abnormally large mouth at first. SCP-XXXX-1 at this stage are seen eating nearby objects. As SCP-XXXX-1 matures, SCP-XXXX-1 will slowly lose the ability to see as fleshy growth will began to cover their eyes, rendering them blind. SCP-XXXX-1 will become more hungry as it matures, causing them to attack nearby creatures or objects and consume them. SCP-XXXX-1 takes around two hours to mature. Observation shows that SCP-XXXX-1 becomes less intelligent as it matures.

If a human subject comes into SCP-XXXX without full body protection (skin exposed, etc), SCP-XXXX will rapidly grow tendons and ensnare them onto the wall. SCP-XXXX will then absorbs the victim onto the wall as subjects goes through a stage of "degradation." Subjects will slowly transform into a fetus as intelligence decreases. After the transformation, subjects will be converted into SCP-XXXX-1, SCP-XXXX-1 made from human subjects will be designated SCP-XXXX-2. SCP-XXXX-2 seems to retain most of their memories at first and suffer from severe polyphagia, making interviewing possible but dangerous. All SCP-XXXX-2 instances must be terminated two hours after their transformation.

SCP-XXXX was discovered at the construction of Area-40 when a group of miners broke into a section of SCP-XXXX, resulting in the release and attack of several SCP-XXXX-1 specimens. All escaped SCP-XXXX-1 instances are terminated and SCP-XXXX is sealed off.

Addendum A, Record of experiments:

- Close file
D-84517 was put into SCP-XXXX to observe it's effect. A camera was put inside SCP-XXXX to monitor D-84517's transformation.

Time: 00:40
D-84517 began struggling and begging for help, great pain reported.

Time: 05:44
D-84517 can be seen shrinking in size as clothes become ripped apart by tendons. Heavy dermal disfigurement can be seen.

Time: 07:01

D-84517's hair dropped off as vocalization becomes more "child like." D-84517's limbs can be seen contorted and disfigured.

Time: 12:55
D-84517's limbs began to fuse on the wall as skin began to flake off, D-84517's is moaning and crying.

Time: 15:06
D-84517 began vomiting blood and other body fluid as vocalization ceases. D-84517 shrunk to around 73.4 cm. Teeth began to fall out.

Time: 20:12
D-84517 became unconscious. At this point, the body began to resemble a baby.

Time: 30:47
[DATA EXPUNGED]

Time: 40:24
D-84517 fully transformed into a fetus.

Time: 55:20
D-84517 began to grow into SCP-XXXX-2, limbs began to grow as torso becomes heavier. A tendril came out of SCP-XXXX and began injecting D-84517 with a yellow fluid.

Time: 01:03:35
D-84517's eyes began to grow thinner as jaws expand. Heavy tumorous growth appeared on the body.

Time: 01:10:23
D-84517 began to grow sharp sets of teeth. A second jaw can be seen emerging from D-84517's mouth.

Time: 01:20:00
D-84517 became conscious again. D-84517 began to grow claws. Blood vessels can be seen popping up.

Time: 01:31:27
D-84517 is fully converted into SCP-XXXX-2 at this point. SCP-XXXX released D-84517 from itself.

- Close file
D-29849 was exposed to SCP-XXXX and was transformed into SCP-XXXX-2. D-29849 was sent back into SCP-XXXX. A tracking device has been planted onto D-29849 in order to monitor D-29845.

Time: 00:15 to 04:00
D-29845 began wandering around SCP-XXXX, occupationally eating the walls of SCP-XXXX while mumbling the word "Thanks, mother."

Time: 04:00 to 07:00
D-29845 entered a hallway lined with unborn SCP-XXXX-1 instances. SCP-XXXX-2 began eating them while mumbling the word "Don't be born."

Time: 07:00 to 09:00
An blind SCP-XXXX-1 specimen attempted to attack and consume D-29845. However, D-29845 broke its jaws and snapped its neck. D-29845 began tearing SCP-XXXX-1 apart.

Time: 09:00 to 12:00
D-29845 attempted to communicate with two other SCP-XXXX-1 specimens. The two SCP-XXXX-1 specimens attempted to mimic D-29845's vocalization. Some stuff D-29845 said was "Les't be brother and sisters before we reach [DATA REDACTED]", "Mother is tasty" and "I was reborn into this." The two SCP-XXXX-1 specimens can be seen "playing" with D-29845 before leaving.

Time: 12:00 to 15:00
An tumorous growth can be seen covering D-29845's eyes, rendering D-29845 blind. D-29845 began eating the walls of SCP-XXXX.

Time: 15:00 to 22:00
D-29845 assaulted an SCP-XXXX-1 specimen. D-29845 was killed and consumed by SCP-XXXX-1, signals was lost one hour later.

ETA 2: Now with 100% more pictures. 'Cave entrance in disused quarry' by Gareth James CC-SA 2.0

ETA 3: 'Fleshwall' from Sly Maria. Used with permission.

Post rewrite/prerepost discussion for interested parties:

+ show block
– hide block

pastarasta1 3 Oct 2016, 22:12

Glad to see this reworked.

My-Off-Day 3 Oct 2016, 22:38

What the ever-loving hell.

(I love it)

+1.

I only have issue with this line:

Site-2385 has been established around the anomaly.

Specifically, the word anomaly. It seems like a weird word placement, but I know it's probably there to make the 2385 redundant. Just throwing it out there.

Madman with Keyboard 3 Oct 2016, 23:05

Why exactly wluld the Foundation draw attention to something linked to this SCP? I mean, I doubt it'll cause a Containment Breach or anything, but by making people aware of the event (reported through Weekly World News or not), it can cause unwated attention.

Also, I don't even want to know what the hell is going on with that Sphere, do I?

Anyways, I'm going to keep this as it is right now. The instances are interesting, but the implied story behind it is just bizzare—what, 2385 are trying to become babies? What, are these things the sperm of some Eldritchian Entity or something, impregnating random women across the globe?

It's interesting, but there's just not enough for me to justify a +1. Still, pretty good.

dankaar 4 Oct 2016, 00:01

Why exactly wluld the Foundation draw attention to something linked to this SCP? I mean, I doubt it'll cause a Containment Breach or anything, but by making people aware of the event (reported through Weekly World News or not), it can cause unwated attention.

If I ran up to you saying that I totally saw Bat Boy, would you belive me?

Hercules Rockefeller 6 Nov 2016, 08:50

That takes me back. Good times.

Shaggydredlocks 3 Oct 2016, 23:30

It's more like them drowning out the actual story with incredible sources documenting it. There's quite a few skips that do similar things.

elias smuts 4 Oct 2016, 01:03

Am I correct in assuming this is a portal to some woman's digestive system?

Check that, reproductive system. Which may make this even creepier.

Well, that's… disturbing, but Subject Alpha was strangely adorable.
Last edited on 4 Oct 2016, 01:20 by elias smuts Show more

TyGently 4 Oct 2016, 13:21

I'm fairly sure that this isn't a representation of a single person's reproductive system, but of some kind of ideal reproductive system for all of humanity (or a subset).

The D-class' words imply that more than just the one worm have entered the "egg", and more will continue to, so my interpretation is that this is some kind of gestation chamber for human souls themselves, maturing and learning before proceeding to be conceived and eventually born.

Shaggydredlocks 4 Oct 2016, 14:43

*Gives Ty an entire box of cookies!*

Lex1nat0r 4 Oct 2016, 10:17

It bugs me a little that apparently the Foundation tried manned exploration before sending in the drones, but that's minor. Definite upvote.

TyGently 4 Oct 2016, 13:18

I initially had a problem with it as well, but the reappearance of the D-class later was both clever and satisfying, coming from someone who finds the typical wastes of D-class very distasteful. It's nice to see one surviving and accomplishing something.

Norixson 4 Oct 2016, 13:36

Weird, creepy and really well executed. Take my upvote!

SleepDelay 4 Oct 2016, 14:11

Nothing and no one can ever take this away

Kid F 4 Oct 2016, 14:28

Well, this is a pretty solid one. Vivid, bizarre, and with a sense of deeper meaning. Although I find the throwing of a D-class at it a bit daft, it paid off in the end.

Genshed 4 Oct 2016, 14:44

The image of the D-class roaming the fleshy tunnels with a machete, doing what needs to be done, reminded me of "Tales of the Fear Agent". You made me care about the fates of anomalous fictional meatworms*.

*Not the Circus variety.

canaryfarmer 4 Oct 2016, 16:19

I loved that the D-class seemed to find new purpose in his life trapped in the labyrinth of human tissue, and seemed to be adapting and handling it all quite well. His verbal interactions with S-A were a delight.

TL333s 4 Oct 2016, 19:15

It was the behavior of the -2's (and frickin' John Carter of Worm Tunnels there) that led me to upvote. That said, what the fuck. :D (I would love it if some indication was given of where this is located, but that's not an obstacle to voting.)

WrongJohnSilver 7 Oct 2016, 11:40

Clear upvote. The last addendum made it perfect.

One nitpick: A-74 or A-47?

pastarasta1 12 Oct 2016, 00:30

I like that the spooky flesh picture is back! I also like the new spooky cave pic!

Doctor Hoffman 18 Oct 2016, 14:52

Hey this is really good! I like how you incorporate the D-Class in there. SPOILERS:
If I understand it properly, SCP-2385-1s and 2s are like human souls or something but 2s are like ready to be put into the uturus and try to convert 1s into 2s so they can be born and the D-Class is helping facilitate this process.

TheMultiverse 5 Nov 2016, 17:03

Wow! Just wow!

+1

A Blessed Feline 21 Nov 2016, 11:23

I really like this, and the ending felt delightfully weird, but why would the Foundation send in a D-class before sending in a drone (Aside from including a totally badass machete-wielding D-class)? Couldn't the same effect be achieved with for example a civilian who got lost into the thing while it was discovered. Also, how can light from a live feed on a screen from the Foundation cause irreversible retinal damage? Wouldn't it just go plain white? Also, why do Foundation drones have such shitty batteries?
Show headcanon

The -1s and -2s are unborn souls that get assigned a mother and when ascending into the orb. Either -1s or -2s are born as SCP-1719-1, which means that we will soon see either an increase (if SCP-1719-1s are SCP-2385-2s due to D-man shoving -2s into the orb) or decrease (If SCP-1719-1s are -2s, that D-man kills before they can get to the orb) of SCP-1719-1 instances in the future.

Also, there was a fertilized egg, but the camera caused Maya's womb to reject the -2 egg because eww. This is why you check your souls for hidden cameras before allowing them to incarnate (Is it really incarnation if they already have flesh? Or just reincarnation?)
Overall, +1

Last edited on 21 Nov 2016, 13:52 by A Blessed Feline Show more

getrobogetrobo 18 Dec 2016, 01:54

00:19 An instance of SCP-2385-1 is laying to the side of the tunnel

"Lying"?

And now I wonder what happened to D-02894 … vanished? Merged with S-A to be reincarnated as the baby? Jfc.

Agent MacLeod 27 Dec 2016, 02:57

This is beautiful. This is.. Christ. I want to know what the big -1 was. I want to know what happens to the ones that get eaten. I want to know what the D-Class has become, in this metaphor. This is great. New favorite SCP
